page: Microsoft Azure M208s_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 208 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 104 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280L processor using the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 2850.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 13.7 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with 4194 GB of capacity, and the instance is premium storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25%. Conversely, multi-core and memory throughput metrics are in the top 10% tier. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 187,934.47 and a passmark CPU score of 84,712.92. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 10,456.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 415.52 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier at 153.96 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M208s_v2 offers high resource density, particularly for memory-bound workloads that can leverage its massive 2.85 TB RAM capacity and high-bandwidth memory architecture. However, the weak single-core performance represents a tradeoff for workloads reliant on single-threaded execution. This server type is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks, high-throughput Redis deployments, and complex software compilation. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure M208s_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server in the MSv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor with 208 dedicated vCPUs (104 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz. It features 2850.0 GB of RAM, 4194 GB of local storage, and is premium storage capable, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a significant tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are in the top 10% tier. Specifically, memory bandwidth reaches 415.52 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and Redis SET operations achieve 153.96 million operations per second. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for massive in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing, whereas it is inefficient for single-threaded workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M208s_v2 is an MSv2 memory-optimized server with 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2850 GB RAM, and 4194 GB local storage. It delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M208s_v2 is an MSv2 memory-optimized server featuring 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2850 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity, high-bandwidth memory throughput, and exceptional multi-core performance for large-scale database workloads.
bullet_points: 208 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors.
2850.0 GB of RAM providing 13.7 GB of memory per core.
4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
Weak single-core performance representing a key architectural tradeoff.
Suited for large-scale in-memory databases and Redis deployments.
